latest.title: Investment Update - Paraytec
latest.content:
21 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1727F
Truetide PLC
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
Truetide plc
("
Truetide
", the "
Company
" or the "
Group
")
Investment update - Paraytec Advances CX300 Programme
Truetide plc (AIM: TRUE) announces that its 100%-owned investee company, Paraytec Limited ("Paraytec"), has completed the proof-of-concept phase of the alpha-synuclein assay development programme announced on 14 January 2026. The CX300 platform successfully demonstrated its ability to measure the aggregation kinetics of recombinant α-synuclein in the presence of oligomer-containing preparations.
This represents a significant step towards a potential high-value clinical screening tool for Parkinson's disease (PD), dementia with Lewy bodies (DLB) and multiple system atrophy (MSA), with the additional potential for monitoring of patient progress.
Alongside the aggregation assay, Paraytec has established a panel of recombinant α-synuclein variants - including the disease-associated A30P and A53T familial Parkinson's mutations and a fluorescently-taggable construct - which the Directors of Paraytec believe creates a compelling platform for comparative strain analysis and discrimination between distinct synucleinopathies.
Following the success of this preliminary project, Paraytec is considering a pilot study to assess rapid (<24-hour) detection of pathological α-synuclein seeding activity in biological samples and capability for detection in clinical samples.
Key Highlights
•
Leverages CX300's real-time fluorescence particle counting to deliver rapid, reproducible aggregation readouts with potential workflow and speed advantages over existing RT-QuIC-based approaches; head-to-head comparison with established platforms will form part of the pilot programme.
•
Proprietary panel of recombinant α-synuclein variants, including A30P, A53T and a site-specifically labelable construct, positions the platform for comparative strain analysis - a capability the Directors believe could support differential diagnosis between PD, DLB and MSA.
•
A successful assay would address the major unmet need for scalable early detection of biomarkers in a neurological diagnostics market projected to reach US$9.2 billion by 2030 (CAGR >10%)*.
•
Multiple potential commercial pathways: near-term research-use-only (RUO) instrument and assay kits; pharma partnership opportunities addressing the drug discovery market for anti-aggregation therapeutics; longer-term LDT/IVD clinical diagnostic tests; intellectual property position across assay format, variant panel and oligomer enrichment methodology will all be actively evaluated.
•
Builds on collaboration with Professor Oliver Bandmann and the Sheffield Institute for Translational Neuroscience (SiTRAN).
Clinical and commercial rationale
Synucleinopathies represent one of the largest underserved diagnostic markets in neurology, with Parkinson's disease alone affecting an estimated 10 million people globally. Current diagnosis relies on phenotypic assessment and imaging, both of which typically detect disease only after substantial neurodegeneration.
Trevor Brown, CEO of Truetide, commented:
"This pilot moves our CX300 platform into the high-value clinical screening arena. We see substantial commercial potential in delivering a rapid, accurate and preferably blood-based test for one of medicine's largest unmet needs. The Board of Paraytec is evaluating options for how best to advance this work and we will update Truetide shareholders as appropriate."
*
https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/neurological-biomarkers-market-153432005.html

Director/PDMR Shareholding - sale of shares
Truetide (AIM: TRUE) has been notified that, on 18 May 2026, Trevor Brown, Chief Executive of the Company, sold 7,500 ordinary shares of 2 pence each in the in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at a price of 2.7 pence per Ordinary Share in the market.
Accordingly, Trevor Brown's total beneficial shareholding in the Company has decreased to 22,132,715 Ordinary Shares, which represents approximately 28.2% of the Company's current voting rights.
Following the sale of Ordinary Shares by Trevor Brown, the Brown Hallam Concert Party, comprising Trevor Brown and Viv Hallam, now has a combined shareholding of 23,541,610 Ordinary Shares, representing 29.99% of the total issued share capital and voting rights of the Company.

company_30s.what_they_do: Truetides Limited develops and commercialises advanced biomolecular assays, with a focus on early detection of neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company’s alpha‑synuclein assay targets a growing diagnostic market that is currently underserved, giving it a unique entry point amid a crowded field of competitors.
company_30s.current_state: Truetides is in the early commercialization phase, having recently launched its Paraytec platform. Cash burn is high as the firm ramps up production, but it has secured a block admission from a larger firm and is moving toward first‑time product sales. The share price is trading at a very low fraction of its pre‑pandemic valuation, reflecting the company’s nascent revenue stream.

rns_narrative: Strategic partnership and product launch: The 14 Jan RNS announced a new assay‑development program with Paraytec, signalling a push into commercial diagnostics.

Capital structure and board realignment: Late‑2025 and early‑2026 RNS detail board changes, sale of non‑core assets, and appointment of a joint corporate broker, indicating a focus on streamlining governance and raising capital.
Financial performance update: The 9 Mar RNS reports a six‑month return and the 4 Feb RNS confirms holdings, suggesting the company is keeping investors informed on liquidity and investor interest.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical curiosity – investors are watching the Paraytec launch but remain cautious.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centres on the potential of the alpha‑synuclein assay to capture a large unmet need; some posts praise the block admission and the company’s pivot toward commercial sales.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the high cash burn, lack of revenue, and the fact that the company has yet to deliver a product to market; they warn that the valuation is unsupported.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past month, sentiment has moved from muted indifference to cautious optimism around the Paraytec program, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no search interest – a blank slate that can turn into a surprise if the company delivers a catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Positive validation of the Paraytec assay in clinical or first‑in‑human trials could spark a rally.
Completion of the block admission – a large firm taking a stake or committing to purchase would signal confidence.
Regulatory filing with the EMA or FDA for the diagnostic platform would be a major milestone.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp uptick in daily volume (e.g., >3× the 3‑month average) and a bounce off the 20‑day moving average; also monitor for any partnership or licensing announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early post‑crash phase; the APEX score of 9.0/21 indicates tight compression that could be primed for a breakout once a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (likely >70%) creates a 100‑200% upside if the company moves from the bottom to the midpoint of its previous high. The recent Paraytec launch is a tangible catalyst that could validate the product pipeline, and historical deep‑bottom signals in similar biotech firms have delivered 160% returns on average.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure additional funding or the Paraytec program stalls, the price could remain stuck at the current low. Cash burn remains a risk, and the lack of revenue means the valuation could collapse further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low (≈0% of the pre‑crash price). Upside could be 100‑200% if the stock recovers to the midpoint of its former high. Risk‑reward ratio is therefore highly asymmetric in favour of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when daily volume spikes above the 3‑month average and the price bounces off the 20‑day moving average. Size the position to 5–10% of available capital and set a stop 5% below entry to protect against a false break.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a confirmed positive result from the Paraytec assay or a partnership announcement; if the price stays flat for 10 days and volume remains low, consider staying out.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risk identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash and a promising assay launch, TRUE.L offers a contrarian upside of 100‑200% if a catalyst triggers mean reversion, but watch for cash burn and lack of revenue as downside risks.
